checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=72893821.08 (FY2014, Q4 implied ~23.5M) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=25573169.26 (Q1 2015), only 8.7% QoQ revenue growth

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=9082048.73 (2013-12-31) -> AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=29596106.52 (2014-12-31), a 226% YoY increase vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of 146% (29,597,324.11 to 72,893,821.08)
evidence.location: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent instant FY2013/FY2014; SalesRevenueGoodsNet annual FY2013/FY2014

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=115671.62 (2014-12-31 and 2015-03-31), essentially flat and immaterial relative to Assets=119468086.53 (2014-12-31)

PropertyPlantAndEquipmentGross=6370462.39 (2011-12-31) grew to 9163627.66 (2015-03-31), roughly in line with overall asset growth from Assets=66082588.93 (2011-12-31) to Assets=123722367.03 (2015-03-31)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=2845521.90 (2014-03-31) grew to InventoryNet=14536878.91 (2015-03-31), a >5x increase in one year

CostOfGoodsSold=2693322.40 (Q1 2014) vs InventoryNet=2845521.90 (2014-03-31, ~95 days); CostOfGoodsSold=5611900.07 (Q1 2015) vs InventoryNet=14536878.91 (2015-03-31, ~233 days) - implied inventory days more than doubled
evidence.location: CostOfGoodsSold and InventoryNet Q1 2014 vs Q1 2015

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic swings from -13472699.99 (FY2012) to 50698263.18 (FY2013) to -2178279.28 (FY2014), while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew steadily from 9556911.18 (FY2012) to 29597324.11 (FY2013) to 72893821.08 (FY2014)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 29596106.52 (2014-12-31) to 38989859.83 (2015-03-31), a 31.7% increase, while the allowance for doubtful accounts remained static

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available from 2008-12-31 through 2015-03-31 for Assets, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic, and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, sufficient for trend analysis, though revenue is tagged inconsistently across SalesRevenueNet/SalesRevenueGoodsNet/Revenues concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature or aggressive revenue recognition leading to accounts receivable growing far faster than revenue (AR +31.7% QoQ vs revenue +8.7% QoQ into Q1 2015), combined with a bad-debt allowance that failed to grow in step with the ballooning receivable balance (allowance frozen at 1,582,874.83 from 2014-12-31 to 2015-03-31 while AR rose by ~$9.4M), suggesting understated allowance for doubtful accounts and potential overstatement of net receivables/revenue.

A large non-cash gain or one-time item (e.g., fair value change on warrants/derivatives, gain on debt extinguishment, or bargain purchase gain) appears to have been recognized in net income during Q4 2013 (net income of $60.6M in a single quarter versus annual revenue of only $29.6M), producing a persistent multi-year divergence between reported net income and operating cash flow (also seen in FY2009-FY2011), consistent with earnings overstatement via non-operating/non-cash gains not supported by cash generation.

Inventory build-up materially outpacing cost of goods sold/revenue growth (implied inventory days rising from ~95 days in Q1 2014 to ~233 days by Q1 2015) suggests potential failure to write down slow-moving or obsolete inventory, or premature capitalization of costs, which would overstate inventory and understate cost of goods sold/inflate gross margin.

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing far faster than revenue in most recent quarters (AR +31.7% QoQ vs revenue +8.7% QoQ, Q4 2014 to Q1 2015), with a static allowance for doubtful accounts ($1,582,874.83) despite the receivable surge

Extreme, unexplained divergence between net income and operating cash flow, including a single-quarter net income of $60,643,587.49 (Q4 2013) against annual operating cash flow of -$16,156,281.62 (FY2013), and similar divergences in FY2009-FY2011

Rapid inventory build-up (InventoryNet growing from $2.85M to $14.5M in one year) with implied inventory days more than doubling, alongside two consecutive years of NT 10-K late-filing notices (2014-03-17 and 2015-03-17) preceding the 10-K filings
